notes: No coordination. Last writer wins. No detection.
Git raised conflicts on textual overlap.
Belief divergences (endpoint paths, schema names) NOT detected — they happen across files git considers independent.
Human must manually resolve every conflict marker. Wall-clock cost: hours.
CI caught approximately 1 of 4 silent overwrites (~30% — the ones that break imports/fixtures).
CI caught approximately 1 of 3 belief divergences (~33% — only the schema-shaped one reliably propagates to test failures).
Wall-clock cost: each crew turn waits for CI (5-15 min in real CI, modeled as 0 here for fairness).
Agents avoided ~2 cross-crew writes by reading coordination.md.
Belief divergences NOT detected — coord.md models file ownership, not semantic decisions inside file content.
Brittle to prompt drift: any system-prompt change can drop compliance to 0.
Wall-clock cost: ~2x of no_synapse (belief-extraction LLM calls).
